Description: Number-guessing game tracking guesses, hints, and best-score records per player.

schema.name: 边的类型
schema.explanation_rich: **Classify a shape by counting its edges.** A `SWITCH` over `边的数量` maps 0→`空`, 1→`点`, 2→`线`, 3→`三角形`, 4→`正方形`, else `--`. Worked example: shape `1` has 3 edges so it resolves to `三角形`; shape `2` has 4 edges so it resolves to `正方形`; shape `3` has 2 edges so it resolves to `线`.

schema.name: 边标签
schema.Description: Multi-value display string of the child edges' values (seeded; one entry per edge, ordered by label).

schema.name: 边长
schema.Description: Multi-value display string of the child edges' values (seeded; one entry per edge, ordered by label).

schema.name: 边角
schema.Description: Multi-value display string of the child edges' values (seeded; one entry per edge, ordered by label).

schema.name: 角度总和
schema.formula: =SUMIFS(边!{{角度}}, 边!{{形状}}, 形状!{{形状Id}})

schema.name: 最大边长
schema.formula: =SUMIFS(边!{{长度}}, 边!{{形状}}, 形状!{{形状Id}})

schema.name: 非斜边角度总和
schema.formula: =SUMIFS(边!{{长度平方}}, 边!{{形状}}, 形状!{{形状Id}})

schema.name: 勾股定理成立
schema.formula:
=IF({{边的数量}} = 3,
    {{非斜边角度总和}} = {{最大边长}} * {{最大边长}},
  BLANK()
)
schema.explanation_rich: **Does the Pythagorean theorem hold for this shape?** Fires only when `边的数量 = 3`. Compares the sum of the *non-hypotenuse* edges' squared lengths (`非斜边角度总和`, which is actually a SUMIFS over `长度平方`) against `最大边长²`. Worked example: shape `1` (3-4-5 triangle) — non-hypotenuse squares sum to 9 + 16 = 25, max edge squared is 5·5 = 25, so the result is TRUE. For non-triangle shapes the rule returns BLANK by design — the theorem is silent outside its domain.

data.边标签: 阿, 比, 西, 迪

形状.summary_rich: The outer table. Three rows. Each row aggregates over the inner table (`边的数量`, `角度总和`, `最大边长`) and runs the headline calculated field `勾股定理成立` — TRUE on some rows, blank on others. The pattern is the puzzle.

schema.name: 形状的最大边长
schema.formula: =INDEX(形状!{{最大边长}}, MATCH(边!{{形状}}, 形状!{{形状Id}}, 0))

schema.name: 边的数量
schema.formula: =INDEX(形状!{{边的数量}}, MATCH(边!{{形状}}, 形状!{{形状Id}}, 0))

schema.name: 是否为斜边
schema.explanation_rich: **Is this edge the hypotenuse of its shape?** Equality check: `长度 = 形状的最大边长`, where `形状的最大边长` is a lookup back to the parent shape. Worked example: in shape `1` (3-4-5 triangle) the edge of length 5 flips TRUE; the edges of length 3 and 4 stay FALSE. Every edge of the square (shape `2`) is TRUE because all four edges tie for longest.

schema.name: 形状类型
schema.formula: =INDEX(形状!{{边的类型}}, MATCH(边!{{形状}}, 形状!{{形状Id}}, 0))

边.summary_rich: The inner table. Each row belongs to one outer row and carries two raw numbers (`长度`, `角度`). One field (`是否为斜边`) is calculated by comparing this row's `长度` to a lookup of the parent's longest. The leaves of the DAG.

Name: Guessing Game

__meta__.Description: Project-level metadata that travels with the rulebook: tagline, motif, narrative descriptions, substrate list, signature rows, etc. One row per metadata key. Use ValueType to interpret StringValue vs JsonValue.

schema.name: MetaKey
schema.Description: The metadata key (e.g. 'tagline', 'motif_palette', 'substrates'). Unique within the table.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Identifier for this metadata entry. Mirrors MetaKey so the row is addressable by Name like every other table.

schema.name: ValueType
schema.Description: How to interpret the value columns: 'string' (use StringValue), 'object' (parse JsonValue as JSON object), 'array' (parse JsonValue as JSON array).

schema.name: StringValue
schema.Description: Plain string value. Populated when ValueType == 'string'; null otherwise.

schema.name: JsonValue
schema.Description: JSON-encoded value. Populated when ValueType == 'object' or 'array'; null when ValueType == 'string'.

data.Name: tagline
data.StringValue: Two tables in 中文. Three rows. One headline rule. Figure out what it's computing.

data.Name: description_rich
data.StringValue: A rulebook deliberately written in **Chinese**. Two tables — `形状` and `边` — with three rows in one and nine in the other. The field names don't tell you what they mean unless you read 中文; the data is just numbers. Somewhere in the schema is a calculated boolean named `勾股定理成立` that fires on some rows and stays blank on others. **What is this rulebook actually computing?** The game is to read enough of the data — without reading the formulas — to guess the answer. Open the rules drawer for the spoilers.

data.Name: use_cases
data.JsonValue: ["**Browse `形状` and `边` blind.** Look at the values. Three rows in one table; nine rows in the other. Two integer columns called `长度` and `角度`. Guess what the rulebook is modeling before reading any formula.", "**Find the column named `勾股定理成立`.** Some rows are `TRUE`, some `FALSE`, some blank. The blank pattern correlates with another field. What's the rule?", "**Edit one row's `长度` and watch every dependent field recompute.** The DAG fires whether you understand 中文 or not — proving the rulebook is the spec and every substrate just witnesses it."]

data.Name: journal_seed
data.StringValue: Three rows in 形状, nine in 边. The fields don't name themselves in English. The rulebook is computing *something* — but what?

data.Name: CMCC_Summary
data.StringValue: Airtable export with intelligent type inference: Schemas, Data, Relationships (FK links), Lookups (INDEX/MATCH), Aggregations (SUMIFS/COUNTIFS/Rollups), and Calculated fields (formulas) in Excel dialect. Field types are inferred from actual data values with priority: DateTime → Decimal → Integer → Boolean → Base64 → JSON → String.

data.Name: conversion_metadata
data.JsonValue: {"source_base_id": "appC6Tnt2ZKm3t16e", "table_count": 2, "tool_version": "2.0.0", "field_type_mapping": "multipleRecordLinks→relationship, multipleLookupValues→lookup, rollup→aggregation, count→aggregation, formula→calculated", "export_mode": "intelligent_type_inference", "type_inference": {"enabled": true, "priority": "data_analysis → formula_analysis → reference_resolution → airtable_metadata", "type_hierarchy": "datetime → number → integer → boolean → base64 → json → string", "nullable_support": true, "error_value_handling": "#NUM!, #ERROR!, #N/A, #REF!, #DIV/0!, #VALUE!, #NAME? are treated as NULL"}}
